svg.title: Enformer's first convolutional layer as a learned bank of PWM matched filters
svg.desc: A two-panel structural correspondence diagram. The left panel shows the classical PWM matched-filter interpretation: a hand-curated 4-by-15 PWM template scanning a 4-channel one-hot DNA signal to produce a single matched-filter response track. The right panel shows the first convolutional layer of Enformer: a bank of 256 learned 4-by-15 convolution kernels operating on the same 4-channel one-hot DNA, producing 256 parallel matched-filter response tracks. The two panels are connected by a wide equivalence arrow and the annotation that the kernels are learned from data instead of being hand-curated, but the underlying operation is identical discrete cross-correlation. Below, three example learned filters are shown with their resemblance to known transcription-factor motifs.

g.text: CNN FIRST LAYER = LEARNED PWM BANK
Same operation as motif scanning; the kernels are trained instead of hand-curated
single hand-curated 4 × L template · one response track
Many trained kernels resemble known TF motifs — CTCF, GATA, SP1, FOX-family — rediscovered without supervision. Others compose into higher-order patterns in subsequent
convolution layers: motif spacing, motif co-occurrence, cooperative-binding signatures. The architecture is matched-filter banks plus learned representations.
